NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: An oracle concerning Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum of Elkosh.
text.Rotherham: The oracle, on Nineveh,—the scroll of the vision of Nahum, the Elkoshite.

text.NRSV: A jealous and avenging God is the Lord, the Lord is avenging and wrathful; the Lord takes vengeance on his adversaries and keeps wrath for his enemies.
text.Rotherham: A GOD jealous and avenging, is Yahweh, an avenger, is Yahweh, and a lord of wrath, an avenger, is Yahweh towards his adversaries, and, a retainer [of anger], is he to his foes.

text.NRSV: The Lord is slow to anger but great in power, and the Lord will by no means clear the guilty. His way is in whirlwind and storm, and the clouds are the dust of his feet.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, is slow to anger, but great in vigour, He will not leave, unpunished, as for Yahweh, in storm-wind and in tempest, is his way, and, clouds, are the dust of his feet.

text.NRSV: He rebukes the sea and makes it dry, and he drains all the rivers; Bashan and Carmel wither, and the bloom of Lebanon fades.
text.Rotherham: Who rebuketh the sea and hath made it dry, and, all the streams, hath he dried up,—withered are Bashan and Carmel, Even the bloom of Lebanon, is withered:

text.NRSV: The mountains quake before him, and the hills melt; the earth heaves before him, the world and all who live in it.
text.Rotherham: Mountains, have trembled because of him, and, the hills, have melted,—and the earth, hath lifted itself up, at his presence, the world also, and all who dwell therein.

text.NRSV: Who can stand before his indignation? Who can endure the heat of his anger? His wrath is poured out like fire, and that rocks are shattered by him.
text.Rotherham: Before his indignation, who shall stand? And who shall abide the glow of his anger? His wrath, hath been poured forth like fire, and, the rocks, have been broken down because of him.

text.NRSV: The Lord is good, a stronghold in a day of trouble; he protects those who take refuge in him,
text.Rotherham: Good is Yahweh, as a protection in the day of distress,—and one who acknowledgeth them who seek refuge in him.

text.NRSV: even in a rushing flood. He will make a full end of his adversaries, and will pursue his enemies into darkness.
text.Rotherham: But, with an overflow rolling on, a full end, will he make of them who rise up against him,—and, his foes, will he pursue into darkness.

text.NRSV: Why do you plot against the Lord? He will make an utter end; trouble will not rise up a second time.
text.Rotherham: What can ye devise against Yahweh? A full end, is he making,—Distress, shall not rise up twice:

text.NRSV: Like entangled thorns, like drunkards as they drink, they are consumed like dry straw.
text.Rotherham: Though they were like thorns intertwined, and as drunkards drenched with their drink, yet have they been devoured, like stubble fully dry.

text.NRSV: From you one has gone out who plots evil against the Lord, one who counsels wickedness.
text.Rotherham: Out of thee, hath one come forth—plotting, against Yahweh, wickedness,—A counselor of the Abandoned One.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, “Though they are at full strength and many, they will be cut off and pass away. Though I have afflicted you, I will afflict you no more.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh, Though they be in full force, and so in great numbers, yet, even so, have they been cut off, and have passed away,—If I humble thee [once], I will not humble thee again.

text.NRSV: “And now I will break off his yoke from you and snap the bonds that bind you.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, will I break his yoke from off thee,—and, thy fetters, will I tear off.

text.NRSV: The Lord has commanded concerning you: “Your name shall be perpetuated no longer; from the house of your gods I will cut off the carved image and the cast image. I will make your grave, for you are worthless.”
text.Rotherham: Then will Yahweh give command concerning thee, None of thy name shall be sown any more,—out of the house of thy gods, will I cut off carved image and molten image, I will appoint thy grave, for thou art of little esteem.

text.NRSV: Look! On the mountains the feet of one who brings good tidings, who proclaims peace! Celebrate your festivals, O Judah, fulfill your vows, for never again shall the wicked invade you; they are utterly cut off.
text.Rotherham: Lo! upon the mountains, The feet of one, who bringeth Good Tidings! who publisheth Prosperity! Celebrate, O Judah, thy pilgrim festivals, fulfil thy vows, for, not again, any more, shall the Abandoned One, pass through thee, he hath been wholly cut off.

text.NRSV: A shatterer has come up against you. Guard the ramparts; watch the road; gird your loins; collect all your strength.
text.Rotherham: He that breaketh in pieces hath come up over thy face, Keep the keeps,—watch the way, brace the loins, make vigour very firm.

text.NRSV: (For the Lord is restoring the majesty of Jacob, as well as the majesty of Israel, though ravagers have ravaged them and ruined their branches.)
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh hath restored the excellency of Jacob, like the excellency of Israel,—for the plunderers have plundered them, and, their vine branches, have they marred.

text.NRSV: The shields of his warriors are red; his soldiers are clothed in crimson. The metal on the chariots flashes like fire on the day of his preparation; the chargers prance.
text.Rotherham: The shield of his heroes, is made red, the men of war, are clad in crimson, on fire, are the chariot-steels, on the day he maketh ready,—and, the lances, are put in motion.

text.NRSV: The chariots race madly through the streets, they rush to and fro through the squares; their appearance is like torches, they dart like lightning.
text.Rotherham: In the streets, madly go the chariots, they rush along in the broadways,—their appearance, is like torches, as lightnings, hither and thither do they run.

text.NRSV: The officers are summoned, they stumble as they go forward; they hasten to the wall, and the mantelet is set up.
text.Rotherham: Let him call to mind his nobles, they shall stumble as they go—let them hasten to her wall, yet the storming cover, is prepared.

text.NRSV: The river gates are opened; the palace quakes.
text.Rotherham: The gates of the rivers, have been opened,—and, the palace, doth quake.

text.NRSV: It is decreed that the city be exiled, its women led away, moaning like doves and beating their breasts.
text.Rotherham: And, Huzzab, hath been taken captive—hath been led up,—and, her handmaids, are making a moan like the sound of doves, as they taber upon their heart.

text.NRSV: Nineveh is like a pool whose waters run away. “Halt! Halt!”—but no one turns back.
text.Rotherham: Yet, as for Nineveh, like a reservoir of waters, are her waters,—but, those men, are in flight! Stand! stand!! but no one is turning.

text.NRSV: “Plunder the silver, plunder the gold! There is no end of treasure! An abundance of every precious thing!”
text.Rotherham: Plunder silver, plunder gold,—and there is no end to the costly furnishing, rich with every article of delight.

text.NRSV: Desolation, devastation, and destruction! Hearts faint and knees tremble, all loins quake, all faces grow pale!
text.Rotherham: Emptiness, yea turned to emptiness, aye deserted [is she] ! with, heart, unnerved, and, a tottering, of knees, and, anguish, in all loins, and, the faces of them all, have withdrawn their colour.

text.NRSV: What has become of the lions’ den, the cave of the young lions, where the lion goes, and the lion’s whelp, with no one to disturb them?
text.Rotherham: Where is the lair of the lions? Yea the very feeding-place of the young lions,—where walked the lion, the lioness, the lion’s whelp, with none to make them afraid?

text.NRSV: The lion has torn enough for his whelps and strangled prey for his lionesses; he has filled his caves with prey and his dens with torn flesh.
text.Rotherham: The lion, used to tear in pieces enough for his whelps, and to strangle for his lionesses,—and then fill with prey his holes, and his lairs with what he had torn.

text.NRSV: See, I am against you, says the Lord of hosts, and I will burn your chariots in smoke, and the sword shall devour your young lions; I will cut off your prey from the earth, and the voice of your messengers shall be heard no more.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! against thee, Declareth Yahweh of hosts, Therefore will I burn up in smoke her chariots, and, thy young lions, shall be devoured by the sword,—so will I cut off, out of the earth, thy prey, nor shall be heard any more, the voice of thine envoy.

text.NRSV: Ah! City of bloodshed, utterly deceitful, full of booty— no end to the plunder!
text.Rotherham: Alas for the city of bloodshed! All of it, deceit, of violence full, none releaseth prey!

text.NRSV: The crack of whip and rumble of wheel, galloping horse and bounding chariot!
text.Rotherham: The sound of the whip, and the sound of the rushing wheel,—and horse galloping, and dancing chariot rattling along.

text.NRSV: Horsemen charging, flashing sword and glittering spear, piles of slain, heaps of corpses, dead bodies without end— they stumble over the bodies!
text.Rotherham: Horsemen uplifting both the flashing sword, and the lightning spear, Aye, a mass of slain, and a weight of dead bridles,—and no end of corpses, so that they stumble upon their corpses.

text.NRSV: Because of the countless debaucheries of the prostitute, gracefully alluring, mistress of sorcery, who enslaves nations through her debaucheries, and peoples through her sorcery,
text.Rotherham: Because of the multitude of the unchaste doings of the unchaste one, fair in grace, mistress of secret arts,—who hath been selling nations by her unchaste doings, families by her secret arts,

text.NRSV: I am against you, says the Lord of hosts, and will lift up your skirts over your face; and I will let nations look on your nakedness and kingdoms on your shame.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! against thee, Declareth Yahweh of hosts, Therefore will I remove thy shirts over thy face,—and let, nations, see thy nakedness, and, kingdoms, thy shame;

text.NRSV: I will throw filth at you and treat you with contempt, and make you a spectacle.
text.Rotherham: And I will cast upon thee abominable filth, and treat thee as foolish,—and set thee as a gazing-stock.

text.NRSV: Then all who see you will shrink from you and say, “Nineveh is devastated; who will bemoan her?” Where shall I seek comforters for you?
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass that, all who see thee, shall flee from thee, and shall say, Destroyed is Nineveh! Who will bemoan her?—Whence shall I seek any to comfort thee?

text.NRSV: Are you better than Thebes that sat by the Nile, with water around her, her rampart a sea, water her wall?
text.Rotherham: Art thou better than No-amon, who sat among the Nile-streams, waters round about her,—whose fortress was the sea, from the sea, her wall.

text.NRSV: Ethiopia was her strength, Egypt too, and that without limit; Put and the Libyans were her helpers.
text.Rotherham: Ethiopia, was her strength, and Egypt—Yea, without end,—Put and Lubim, were among thy helpers.

text.NRSV: Yet she went into exile, she went into captivity; her infants were dashed in pieces at the head of every street; lots were cast for her nobles, and all her dignitaries were bound in fetters.
text.Rotherham: Yet, she, was given up to exile, she went into captivity, even her babes, were dashed to the ground, at the head of all the streets,—and, for her honourable men, cast they lots, and, all her great men, were bound together in chains.

text.NRSV: You also will be drunken, you will go into hiding; you will seek a refuge from the enemy.
text.Rotherham: Thou too, shalt be drunken, thou shalt hide thyself,—thou too, shalt seek shelter from the foe:

text.NRSV: All your fortresses are like fig trees with first-ripe figs— if shaken they fall into the mouth of the eater.
text.Rotherham: All thy fortresses, shall be fig-trees with first-ripe figs: if thy be shaken, then shall [the fruit] fall on the mouth of the eater.

text.NRSV: Look at your troops: they are women in your midst. The gates of your land are wide open to your enemies; fire has consumed your bars.
text.Rotherham: Lo! thy people, are women, in thy midst, to thy foes, have been set wide open the gates of thy and,—a fire, hath devoured, thy bars.

text.NRSV: Draw water for the siege, strengthen your forts; get into the clay, tread the mortar, take hold of the brick mold!
text.Rotherham: Siege-water, draw for thyself, strengthen thy fortresses,—go into the clay, and tread thou the mortar, make strong the brick.

text.NRSV: There the fire will devour you, the sword will cut you off. It will devour you like the locust. Multiply yourselves like the locust, multiply like the grasshopper!
text.Rotherham: There, shall a fire devour thee, the sword, shall cut thee off, it shall devour thee like the grass locust, make thyself numerous like the grass locust, make thyself numerous as the swarming locust:

text.NRSV: You increased your merchants more than the stars of the heavens. The locust sheds its skin and flies away.
text.Rotherham: Though thou have multiplied thy foot-soldiers beyond the stars of the heavens, the grass locust, hath stript itself and flown away!

text.NRSV: Your guards are like grasshoppers, your scribes like swarms of locusts settling on the fences on a cold day— when the sun rises, they fly away; no one knows where they have gone.
text.Rotherham: Thy mercenary crowds, are like the swarming locust, and, thy mixed multitudes, like locusts—swarms of locusts,—which settle in the hedges on a cold day, the sun, hath broken forth, and they are in flight, and unknown is the place where they are!

text.NRSV: Your shepherds are asleep, O king of Assyria; your nobles slumber. Your people are scattered on the mountains with no one to gather them.
text.Rotherham: Asleep are thy shepherds, O king of Assyria, thy nobles, must needs rest. Scattered are thy people upon the mountains, and there is none to gather them.

text.NRSV: There is no assuaging your hurt, your wound is mortal. All who hear the news about you clap their hands over you. For who has ever escaped your endless cruelty?
text.Rotherham: No lessening of thine injury, grievous is thy wound,—all who have heard the report of thee, have clapped their hands over thee, for, upon whom, hath not thy cruelty passed without ceasing?
